response:
# Verdict: REJECT ## Overall Assessment This Mega Menu implementation has fundamental accessibility violations that block keyboard navigation entirely, prevent screen reader users from understanding menu state changes, and use non-semantic HTML throughout. The component fails to implement any WAI-ARIA pattern correctly — it's not even 80% implemented; critical ARIA attributes are completely absent. This is a design-level failure requiring complete restructuring before implementation can proceed. ## Pre-commitment Predictions (Before Reading Code) Based on the component type, I predicted: 1. **CRITICAL**: Menu items using divs instead of buttons/links — would fail WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value 2. **MAJOR**: Missing `aria-expanded` state communication for collapsible menus — screen readers wouldn't know when submenu opens/closes (WCAG 4.1.3) 3. **CRITICAL**: No keyboard event handlers (`onKeyDown`) — arrow key navigation impossible (WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard) 4. **MAJOR**: Submenu using divs instead of semantic list/role="menu" structure (WAI-ARIA Menu pattern incomplete) **Actual findings matched predictions exactly.** The code comments even acknowledge these bugs, but the implementation is worse than expected — no focus management logic exists at all in this component. --- ## Critical Findings (Blocks Access Entirely) ### 1. No Navigation Landmark ```jsx
// Line:
wrapper has no role attribute ``` - **File**: `src/components/MegaMenu.tsx` (inferred path based on fixture context) - **WCAG/APG**: WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships; WAI-ARIA Navigation pattern requires `
` or `role="navigation"` with proper landmark structure - **User Group Impacted**: Screen reader users cannot identify this as navigation content, breaking page orientation - **Fix Required**: Wrap in `
` at minimum. If multiple navs exist on page, use descriptive labels like "Primary Navigation" ### 2. Clickable Divs Without Button Semantics or Keyboard Support ```jsx
setOpenMenu(...)}> // Lines: menu-item div with only onClick handler {item.label} ...
